title: RETURNING old.* seg-faults
poc: CREATE TABLE a (id INTEGER NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY);
UPDATE a SET id = a.id + 1 FROM (SELECT * FROM a) AS old RETURNING old.*;
update x set a=c from y where b=d returning *;
update x set a=c from y where b=d returning x.*;
update x set a=c from y where b=d returning y.*;
update x set a=c from y where b=d returning x.a, y.d;
INSERT INTO t1 VALUE(1) RETURNING t1.*;
key_bug_features: RETURNING with table.* causes segmentation fault
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE users(id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL, age INTEGER);
UPDATE users SET age = age + 1 WHERE name = 'Alice' RETURNING users.*;
CREATE TABLE orders(order_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, user_id INTEGER, amount REAL);
INSERT INTO orders(user_id, amount) VALUES(1, 99.99) RETURNING orders.*;
